reasoning: The FRED trend analysis explicitly gives a combined probability of 0.64 for the Nov 2, 2025 horizon, serving as a robust anchor. As the forecast horizon extends to November 25 and January 24, the overarching macroeconomic condition — an ongoing Fed easing cycle — exerts a gradual downward pressure on T-bill yields, lowering the likelihood of values remaining elevated above the 3.65 threshold. Following instructions to increase uncertainty for distant horizons (moving probabilities closer to 0.5) and reflecting this downward macro pull, the probabilities decrease from 0.64 near-term, to 0.56 medium-term, and cross slightly to 0.49 for the long-term, strictly minimizing the distance to 0.5 as time increases.
